title: Automated Designation Process - Swap of Swap
toc_path: Automated Designation Process - Swap of Swap
body_html:
Automated Designation Process - Swap of Swap

An FX swap can also roll over or shorten a hedging relationship that is already derived from an FX swap. The swap of swap follows the rules of the automated designation process of FX swaps.

See also: Automated Designation Process - FX Swap

Example

We create a second FX swap for a hedging relationship (which already contains three hedging instruments).

The following two scenarios depending on the concrete swap date can occur for swapping to a later date:

Exposure Prolongation from t3 to t4

  • Rollover, swap date before the t2

    All previous hedging instruments are involved in the prolongation process. On t2-x the new hedging relationship B is created. In the hedging relationship B, five hedging instruments and one hedged item exist.

    Dedesignation Flows:

    • One dedesignation flow is created for the end date (t4) of the second leg of the new FX swap for hedging relationship B.

    • One dedesignation flow is created for the prolongation date (t2-x) for the second leg of the hedging instrument of hedging relationship A.

    • One dedesignation flow is created for the prolongation date (t2-x) for the original hedging instrument of hedging relationship A.

  • Rollover, swap date after the t2

    We have a behavior similar to the normal swap. On t2+x the new hedging relationship is created. In the hedging relationship, three hedging instruments (the second leg of the old hedging relationship and the two new FX forwards of the second FX swap) and one hedged item exist.

    Two dedesignations are needed: One dedesignation is created at the end date (t4) of the second leg of the new FX swap and also one dedesignation for the second leg of the hedging relationship to be swapped and the new offsetting hedging instrument.

The following two scenarios depending the concrete swap date can occur for swapping to an earlier date:

Exposure Shortening from t3 to t3-x

In case of a shortening FX swap we still have only one dedesignation for the exposure subitem and all the hedging instruments involved at the end of the first leg t3-x.
